## 3. Executed Command

```bash
nc_chipgen \
    --soc-config  "/tmp/soc_config_gen.yaml" \
    --out          "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526" \
    --pdk-root     "/nc/apps/pdk" \
    --nc-lib-root  "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/ip" \
    --core-width   4500 \
    --core-height  4000
```

### Notes on Command Construction

1. **`generate` subcommand:** The installed `nc_chipgen` (v0.1.0) is a direct
   invocation tool — it has no `generate` subcommand. The generation was
   performed with the equivalent direct call, which executes all 8 pipeline
   stages identically.

2. **`--scenario` flags:** `nc_chipgen` does not accept `--scenario` arguments.
   The 21 Stage-2 scenario files were instead staged into
   `mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/verification/scenarios/` and
   `verification/manifest.yaml` was written post-generation.

3. **`--core-width/--core-height`:** Manual core dimensions were required
   because the default I/O-limited sizing (desired_margin=100 µm) produced
   a core area too small for 32 × CF_SRAM_1024x32 macros (128 KB SRAM).
   The geometry for 32 macros in a 6-column × 6-row grid requires
   usable_core ≥ 3407 × 2921 µm. A 4500 × 4000 µm input yields a
   padframe die of 4700 × 4200 µm and a core.def usable area of
   ~3729 × 3229 µm — sufficient for the 6×6 arrangement.

4. **`build_fw_smoke: false`:** The firmware smoke build was disabled in the
   generation config because `riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc` is not present in this
   environment. The original `config/soc_config.yaml` is unchanged; the
   temporary generation config was `/tmp/soc_config_gen.yaml`.

### Return Code

```
nc_chipgen exit code: 0  (SUCCESS)
```
